spec_match.rationale: Draw-then-replay pattern that surfaces offsetX/Y per event in the event log — demonstrates the Chrome 130 preservation.

interactive.rationale: Two side-by-side canvases, draw on one, replay on the other, event log shows each dispatched PointerEvent's coordinates.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises draw + replay + log showing preserved offsetX/Y — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Records and replays well but no probe distinguishes pre-130 vs post-130 behaviour. The log just shows the post-130 values; a visitor on an older Chrome wouldn't see different output.

references.rationale: No chromestatus.com/feature/5106728480538624 link in the page. Rule #3.

design_system.rationale: Uses one raw hex #3b82f6 (looks like a duplicate of --accent-blue). Should be sourced from var(--accent-blue).

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: Page references the feature but doesn't carry the chromestatus.com/feature/5106728480538624 link. Rule #3 — every page must include it.

openQuestions.title: Replace #3b82f6 with var(--accent-blue)
openQuestions.detail: Raw hex color forbidden by CLAUDE.md rule #5. Switch to the CSS variable so it follows any future theme update.

openQuestions.title: Add an assertion-style summary at the end of replay
openQuestions.detail: The event log lists raw offsetX/Y but doesn't compare them to the original recorded values. A small 'all coordinates match' / 'some retargeted' verdict line at the end of replay would turn the inspector from a viewer into a tester.
